name: Moist Pie Crust Mix
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour and salt in a very large bowl. Mix well.
2. With a pastry blender, cut in shortening until evenly distributed. The mixture should resemble cornmeal in texture.
3. Add cold water all at once and mix lightly until the flour absorbs all the water and the texture resembles putty. If the dough is too sticky, sprinkle additional flour over the top and mix until the dough barely holds together in the bowl.
4. Divide the dough into 10 oblong rolls. Wrap each roll well with plastic wrap and heavy foil.
5. Freeze and label as Moist Pie Crust Mix. Use within 12 months.
Makes 10 rolls of mix, enough for 10 double pie crusts or 20 single pie crusts.
Moist Pie Crust:
1. Partially thaw 1 roll of the mix.
2. Divide the dough into 2 balls. Roll out the dough to the desired thickness between 2 sheets of lightly floured wax paper.
3. Place the dough in a 9-inch pie plate without stretching it.
4. Flute the edges. If the filling recipe calls for a baked pie crust, preheat the oven to 425°F (220°C).
5. Bake 10 to 15 minutes until very lightly browned. Cool.
6. Fill and bake according to directions for the filling. For a double-crust pie, place the top crust over the filling and flute the edges, cutting slits in the top crust.
Makes enough for one 9-inch double-crust pie or two 9-inch single-crust pies.
